source_jobs.title: Family Camp Head Lifeguard
source_jobs.description:
SPEND YOUR SUMMER MAKING AN IMPACT

Oakland Feather River Camp isn’t only a summer job - we are a community that cares, collaborates, and welcomes everyone to access the magic of nature. Summer at OFRC is fast-paced, joyful, challenging, and incredibly rewarding. If you want work that’s meaningful, outdoors, and full of real connection, you’re in the right place!



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/57d17cb2-4eea-47a9-9201-c99a52f88c42/85e09fea-e699-4e3e-a19e-a0c272fc4d5b/cropped%20jump.jpg]



What You’ll Get at OFRC

This is a real job with real responsibility - and real support.

 - Free housing, meals, laundry, utilities and camp store discounts.

 - Highly competitive, transparent pay.

 - Five-day work week with true time off - a rarity in the summer camp world.

 - Professional leadership training.

 - A team culture built on respect, communication, and accountability.

 - A diverse, inclusive community where different identities, backgrounds, and experiences are valued.

 - A chance to create unforgettable experiences in nature for families.

We work hard here. We take care of each other. And we make space for joy, rest, and real connection.




PROGRAM SPECIALIST - HEAD LIFEGUARD - SUMMER 2026

Oakland Feather River Camp | Quincy, CA
$776-$876 per week | Seasonal | On-site | Free Housing and Meals




ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Program Specialist - Head Lifeguard, you ensure the waterfront runs safely and smoothly and oversee the lifeguard team. You coach, train, check skills, manage safety policies, and help with overall Program Department needs when not on the water. This role keeps our waterfront safe, fun, and organized for campers and staff all summer.

You will spend your summer leading the lifeguard team, running trainings, checking gear, watching water conditions, and supporting other program areas to help camp operate well.

Full Job Description: Program Specialist - Head Lifeguard - 2026 Job Description https://featherrivercamp.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Program-Specialist-Head-Lifeguard-Job-Description-2026.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Guard the swim area and watch for safety concerns.

 - Oversee lifeguard operations at the waterfront.

 - Run skills checks and lead in-service training for lifeguards and observers.

 - Track equipment and water conditions, and keep supplies organized.

 - Communicate safety standards and support waterfront staff coaching.

 - Help campers with waterfront info and questions.

 - Lead camp activities when the swimming hole is closed.

Your work helps make the waterfront a safe and fun part of camp for every camper and staff member.




WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 18 or older.

 - At least 2 years of lifeguard experience.

 - Lifeguard certified or ability to get certified.

 - Strong leadership and clear communication skills.

 - Organized, safety-mindful, and calm in emergency situations.

 - Comfortable enforcing rules in a kind, clear way.

 - Works well with a team and enjoys helping others.

 - Someone dependable who shows up on time and works hard.

We treat staff like adults. We expect people to show up fully, work hard, communicate clearly, and support one another.




THE RIGHT FIT

If you take charge of safety, enjoy leading a team, and want to help campers have a great waterfront experience - this role could be perfect for you!




OUR CULTURE & HIRING PHILOSOPHY

At OFRC, we work hard, support each other, and build a community where everyone belongs. We value people who show up with heart, effort, curiosity, and a willingness to learn - no matter their background or experience level. We welcome candidates from traditional and nontraditional paths, and we believe diversity makes our team stronger. You don’t need to meet every single qualification to be a strong candidate here; if this role feels right for you, we encourage you to apply and tell us what you’d bring to our community.

This is a real job with real responsibility - and real support.

 - Five-day work week with true time off - a rarity in the summer camp world.

 - A team culture built on respect, communication, and accountability.

 - A diverse, inclusive community where different identities, backgrounds, and experiences are valued.

 - A chance to create unforgettable experiences in nature for families.

We work hard here. We take care of each other. And we make space for joy, rest, and real connection.




PROGRAM LEADER - LIFEGUARD - SUMMER 2026

Oakland Feather River Camp | Quincy, CA
$696 to $796 per week | Seasonal exempt | On-site | Free housing and meals




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Program Leader - Lifeguard, you help keep the waterfront safe and fun for campers. You guard the swimming area, support and lead daily activities, and help camp run smoothly outside of swim times. This role is active, steady, and a big part of the camp experience.

You will spend your summer outdoors, working with a supportive team, meeting new people, and helping campers feel confident and safe in the water.

Full Job Description: Program Leader - Lifeguard - 2026 Job Description https://featherrivercamp.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Program-Leader-Lifeguard-Job-Description-2026.pdf




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Guard the swim area and watch for safety concerns.

 - Enforce rules and respond when help is needed.

 - Set up and clean up the waterfront each day.

 - Lead camp activities when the swimming hole is closed.

 - Assist with camp-wide programs, events, and weekly changeover tasks.

 - Keep equipment clean, organized, and ready to use.

Your work helps campers feel safe, included, and excited to be at the water every day.




WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 18 or older.

 - Strong swimmer with comfort in moving, natural bodies of water.

 - Lifeguard certified or ability to get certified.

 - Comfortable enforcing rules in a kind, clear way.

 - Stays calm and focused in emergency situations.

 - Works well with a team and enjoys helping others.

 - Someone dependable who shows up on time and works hard.

We treat staff like adults. We expect people to show up fully, work hard, communicate clearly, and support one another.




THE RIGHT FIT

If you care about safety, enjoy working outdoors, and want a summer job where you help campers feel safe and have fun, this could be the right role for you!




OUR CULTURE & HIRING PHILOSOPHY

At OFRC, we work hard, support each other, and build a community where everyone belongs. We value people who show up with heart, effort, curiosity, and a willingness to learn - no matter their background or experience level. We welcome candidates from traditional and nontraditional paths, and we believe diversity makes our team stronger. You don’t need to meet every single qualification to be a strong candidate here; if this role feels right for you, we encourage you to apply and tell us what you’d bring to our community.

COOK - SUMMER 2026

Oakland Feather River Camp | Quincy, CA
$19.40-$21.90 per hour | Seasonal | On-site | Free housing and meals




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Cook at camp, you help prepare meals, keep the kitchen organized, and support the flow of each day. You follow menu plans, prep ingredients, cook food, and help serve campers and staff. You stay active, work with a friendly team, and help keep camp running well for everyone.

This role gives you a chance to build kitchen skills, work outdoors, and be part of a team that supports each other.

Full Job Description: Cook - 2026 Job Description https://featherrivercamp.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Cook-Job-Description-2026.pdf




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Prep and cook meals based on the menu.

 - Help with chopping, mixing, portioning, and cooking tasks.

 - Keep cooking areas clean and follow food safety rules.

 - Set up meal service and help clean up afterward.

 - Restock food and supplies when needed.

 - Watch inventory and let supervisors know when items run low.

 - Wash dishes and help with cleaning as needed.

 - Support the kitchen team with other tasks as needed.

Your work helps make sure meals are ready on time and that the kitchen runs well each day.




WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - Someone who shows up on time and works hard.

 - Comfortable working in a busy kitchen with early mornings and meal-time rushes.

 - 18 or older and able to lift, stand, bend, and move throughout the day.

 - Has some cooking or food prep experience.

 - Communicates clearly and checks in when unsure.

 - Stays steady and positive when things get busy.

We treat staff like adults. We expect people to show up fully, work hard, communicate clearly, and support one another.



THE RIGHT FIT

If you enjoy cooking, like working with a team, and want a summer job where your work helps people every day, this could be the right fit for you!




OUR CULTURE & HIRING PHILOSOPHY

At OFRC, we work hard, support each other, and build a community where everyone belongs. We value people who show up with heart, effort, curiosity, and a willingness to learn - no matter their background or experience level. We welcome candidates from traditional and nontraditional paths, and we believe diversity makes our team stronger. You don’t need to meet every single qualification to be a strong candidate here; if this role feels right for you, we encourage you to apply and tell us what you’d bring to our community.

notes: Summer camp jobs with no tech roles
